---
title: Gaussian Elimination
description: Row-reducing to (reduced) row-echelon form, and the three possible solution
  outcomes.
categories:
- Linear Algebra
---

**Gaussian elimination** uses elementary row operations to reduce a matrix to (reduced) row-echelon form. Every matrix is equivalent, via row operations, to a **unique** RREF.

A system then has one of three outcomes, read directly off the RREF:

- **No solution** — a row of the form $(0\ \cdots\ 0 \mid c)$ with $c \neq 0$, i.e. the contradiction $0 = c$.
- **Unique solution** — every variable is a pivot. For example,

$$
\begin{cases} x + y - z = 2 \\ x - y + z = 4 \\ 3x + 2y - z = 5 \end{cases}
\;\Rightarrow\; x = 3,\ y = -3,\ z = -2
$$

- **Infinitely many** — at least one free (non-pivot) variable, used as a parameter:

$$
\left(\begin{array}{ccc|c} 1 & 2 & -1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 & 0 \\ 5 & 8 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right) \xrightarrow{\text{GE}} \left(\begin{array}{ccc|c} 1 & 0 & 5 & -2 \\ 0 & 1 & -3 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right)
\;\Rightarrow\; \{ (-5t - 2,\ 3t + 1,\ t) \mid t \in \mathbb{R} \}
$$
